hypothesis: If MatAgent augments LLMs with explicit tool access (short-/long-term memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and closed-loop feedback from DiffCSP and iComFormer, then it will more successfully generate novel, valid, and unique inorganic compositions and structures that meet target formation energies (within ±0.25 eV/atom) and yield higher V.U.N. scores than tool-free LLM baselines and MatterGen, because iterative tool-guided planning and property-informed structure refinement improve search efficiency and accuracy.
